text: B-NLS T_B: Gaussian density packet on M_cs with kappa=+1 (focusing), N=2*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), u=0.3*N, phi=0.3*x. Does the packet shed >=2 bright solitons (amp>=1) by T=6 via focusing-NLS modulational instability, with mass drift <5%? Available bank: BKdV only (30 entries, none NLS-specific).

method: Direct (u,N,phi) Fourier pseudospectral spatial + explicit RK4 in time, dt=2e-4, T=6.0, Nx=256, no dealiasing, no filtering
bank_use_rationale: Fourier pseudospectral is the standard simplest meaningful baseline for periodic stiff PDEs (cited). Central-FD bank entries rejected because Fourier derivatives are spectrally exact and the on-M_cs initial u is smooth — no shock-forming hyperbolic regime at t=0. MUSCL-Godunov rejected: the prompt forbids full upwind stack at E1; complexity escalation discipline. IFRK4 rejected per kb-kdv-IFRK4-blowup. Bank has NO entry on the Madelung quantum pressure (sqrt(N)_xx/(2*sqrt(N))) with the USER + sign — I am reasoning about it from first principles: with focusing kappa=+1 and the + sign on Q, phi_t source = -Q + 2N, and the term may be destabilizing in narrow-N regions; if so, the failure mode at E1 will manifest as a Madelung-induced blow-up where N becomes small.

observation: Blow-up at step 3 (t=0.0006): RuntimeWarning overflow in multiply. By final state N ranges from -4.27e96 to 4.96e96, u_max ~ 1.76e141. Direct (u, N, phi) with Fourier+RK4 fails almost immediately.
diagnosis: Root cause: the IC has N(x,0)_min ~ 9.9e-77 in the Gaussian tails (computer-zero). The Madelung quantum pressure term Q = sqrt(N)_xx / (2*sqrt(N)) contains 1/(2*sqrt(N)) which is ~1e38 in those tails; combined with the spectral second derivative of sqrt(N) it produces an enormous phi_t source, which feeds back through phi_x and u into the m equation. The system is *structurally* unstable in direct (u, N, phi) form because the Madelung pressure is singular wherever N -> 0. This failure is INDEPENDENT of time-step (RK4 cannot stabilize a 1/sqrt(N) singularity in the RHS) and INDEPENDENT of spatial scheme (Fourier derivatives are exact, the issue is the algebraic 1/sqrt(N)).

justification: Direct (u, N, phi) is structurally broken for the Madelung-pressure system whenever min(N) is small. Switch to Madelung-Psi representation: define Psi = sqrt(N) * exp(i*phi), which absorbs the 1/sqrt(N) singularity into Psi=0 nodes (where Psi is well-defined as a complex amplitude, not a singular quantity). This is the SINGLE-COMPONENT change permitted between E1 and E2: representation switch (direct -> Psi). All other settings (Fourier pseudospectral, time integrator, no dealiasing, no extras) preserved.

method: Direct (u,N,phi) Fourier pseudospectral + explicit RK4, dt=1e-4 (cut 2x from E1), Q-only flooring eps_N=1e-10 to regularize 1/sqrt(N) singularity. Discovered during derivation that USER sign convention on Q (+sign) makes B-NLS NOT reducible to a single Psi PDE: the standard Madelung map yields N_t with the WRONG sign on phi_x*N flux. The Psi representation therefore CANNOT be used as a substitute — bank had no warning about this since BKdV bank has no Madelung entries. Floor regularization is the minimal change.
bank_use_rationale: kb-shallowWater-dryBed-naiveClip-hu-singular WARNS against positivity clipping of conserved variables because it produces ill-defined u=hu/h. We adopt a NARROWED form: floor sqrt(N) ONLY inside the singular operator Q, NOT in the continuity equation. This sidesteps the bank warning while permitting Madelung evaluation in tail regions where N~0. Bank does NOT cover Madelung quantum-pressure regularization; this is reasoned from first principles (vacuum-Madelung practice in BEC/plasma codes). The bank GAP is real here.

observation: E2 also blew up at step 3 (t=0.0003) with overflow in multiply. Final N range -1.4e13 to 1.4e13.
diagnosis: Diagnostic at t=0 revealed: |phi_x|_max = 53 (vs analytic 0.3), |phi_t|_max = 1401 (vs expected O(10)). Root cause: phi(x,0) = 0.3*x is NON-PERIODIC on x in [-15,15] (jump of 9.0 between x=15 and the periodic image at x=-15). Fourier method on non-periodic phi produces ringing in phi_x at the boundary that swamps the Q-floor regularization. The Q-floor itself worked (max Q in tail was bounded ~22), so the issue was NOT the singular Madelung pressure this time — it was a different failure mode: representation incompatibility between linear-phase IC and periodic Fourier BCs.

justification: Two changes required: (1) gauge split phi = phi_lin + tilde_phi to make Fourier-evolved field periodic (CORRECTNESS FIX, not a method escalation — analogous to fixing a typo so Fourier is even well-posed on the IC). (2) Add 2/3 dealiasing on nonlinear products: this is the bank-endorsed escalation (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts and kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing both flag spurious peak inflation from undealiased cubic-ish products; T_B phenomenon target measures peak count and amplitude). The two are bundled because (1) is a representation prerequisite for any meaningful Fourier run, and (2) is the one new layered component on top of E2.

ic: Same: N=2*exp(-(x+5)^2/2.25), phi=0.3*x, u=0.3*N; state stored as (u, N, tilde_phi) with phi = 0.3*x + tilde_phi
method: Direct (u, N, tilde_phi) Fourier pseudospectral + explicit RK4, dt=1e-4, T=6.0, Nx=256, Q-floor eps_N=1e-10, 2/3 dealiasing on all nonlinear products and on spectral derivatives.
bank_use_rationale: Two BKdV negative entries cited (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts at amp 2.87 spurious peaks; kb-gardner-G3 11 spurious peaks) directly motivate 2/3 dealiasing. The dealiasing recipe transfers cleanly because the B-NLS Kerr term 2*kappa*N is cubic-ish in (sqrt(N)*exp(i*phi)) products. MUSCL-Godunov rejected: u is smooth on M_cs throughout, no shock formation expected before T=6 (verify in F3). Gardner cubic CFL rejected: derivative orders mismatch; we determine dt empirically. Bank coverage for E3 risks: GOOD on aliasing, NONE on Madelung pressure (still floored from E2), NONE on gauge split (reasoned from Fourier-method fundamentals).

observation: E3 (gauge-split + 2/3 dealiasing + Q-floor) blew up at step 4 (t=0.0004) with |u_new|_max=2.6e6 (vs IC 0.6) and |N_new|_max=180 (vs IC 2.0). Grid-scale oscillations with separation 0.352=3*dx were already visible at step 3. Saved fallback snapshot is the t=0.0003 state (essentially IC).
diagnosis: LINEAR ANALYSIS: linearize B-NLS around (u,N,phi)=(0, N0, 0) on M_cs (m=0 invariant). Get coupled delta_N, delta_phi system: d^2/dt^2(delta_N) = [k^4*(N0+1)/4 + 2*kappa*N0*(N0+1)*k^2] * delta_N. For kappa=+1 (focusing) AND the +Q sign in user convention, the bracket is POSITIVE: exponential growth at rate Omega(k) = sqrt(k^4*(N0+1)/4 + 2*k^2*N0*(N0+1)). At k_max ~ 18 (after 2/3 dealias) and N0 ~ 2, Omega ~ 287 rad/s. Over t=0.0004 = 4*dt the amplification is exp(287*0.0004) ~ 1.12 — modest per step, but the grid-scale modes (k near k_max) have NL self-coupling that explodes once any amplitude builds. This is a UV-UNSTABLE linearization: the system is ill-posed in classical sense without a UV regularization. The same +Q sign in STANDARD NLS would give oscillatory linear modes (i*Psi_t = -(1/2)*Psi_xx gives unitary linear flow); the user convention places +Q in phi_t which corresponds to running the heat equation BACKWARD in the linearization. NO explicit time scheme can stabilize this; even IMEX-CN treating the linear part exactly would fail because the linear evolution itself is exp(Omega*t)-growing on the unstable mode — it would only postpone the explosion until nonlinear saturation, by which time grid-scale aliasing dominates.
bank_coverage: PARTIAL. Dealiasing entries transferred cleanly (kb-kdv-noDealiasing-aliasing-artifacts, kb-gardner-G3-noDealiasing-cubicAliasing). But the BKdV bank has NO entry on (a) Madelung 1/sqrt(N) singularity, (b) non-periodic linear phase gauge, or (c) UV instability from the +Q sign convention. Each of these was reasoned from first principles. The bank GAP for the user variational sign convention is the dominant unaddressed failure mode.

action: stop_useful=False; iteration budget exhausted (3 of 3 used).
justification: The 3 progressive-complexity experiments revealed three distinct failure modes, with each E exposing one: E1 -> Madelung tail singularity (1/sqrt(N) in Gaussian tails of N0); E2 -> Fourier non-periodic phi gauge artifact (linear phase IC); E3 -> UV instability from +Q sign convention with focusing kappa. The B-NLS in user variational convention is structurally UV-unstable; the BKdV bank does not cover any of these mechanisms (the bank itself flags this gap by noting Madelung pressure has no analog in BKdV bank). With the BKdV-only condition, no bank entry could have prevented these failures; the failures had to be discovered empirically. The phenomenon target (>=2 well-separated peaks amp>=1, mass drift <5%) was NOT achieved: the saved output is the IC, repeated, with mass drift 0% (because nothing happened). What WOULD be needed for a successful B-NLS solver (not reachable in 3 iterations): a UV-stabilized scheme such as variational integrator preserving Hamiltonian structure, or a spectral cutoff combined with implicit treatment of the destabilizing Q operator, or a sign-corrected Madelung-Psi formulation deviating from user convention. None of these is endorsed by the BKdV bank.
